title: Collapse tldr-cli/remaining/vuln.rs Python path onto canonical compute_taint_with_tree

loc_delta_components.deletions: ~984 LOC: TaintSource const-pattern struct + PYTHON_SOURCES (~75 LOC, 11 entries) + TaintSink const-pattern struct + PYTHON_SINKS (~140 LOC, 17 entries) + TaintTracker struct + impl + TaintInfo struct (~60 LOC) + analyze_python_file + 9 recursive helpers analyze_node/analyze_function/analyze_block/analyze_statement/analyze_assignment/analyze_augmented_assignment/analyze_expression/analyze_call/check_xss_return (~480 LOC) + 7 helpers get_python_parser/node_text/is_taint_source/is_taint_sink/is_parameterized_query/is_string_interpolation_tainted/find_taint_in_string (~165 LOC) + 4 obsolete unit tests test_is_taint_source/test_is_taint_sink/test_taint_tracker/test_string_interpolation_detection (~64 LOC) + 4 unused imports (HashMap retained for build_summary) + module-doc TIGER-04 reference + MAX_TAINT_DEPTH const
loc_delta_components.additions: ~12 LOC: rewrite of analyze_file dispatch (drop .py special case, route through canonical scan_vulnerabilities) + extended doc-comment explaining the M4 collapse rationale + retention-rationale comment in tests mod

stop_thresholds_status.cargo_clippy_d_warnings_passes: PASS — no dead_code, no unused imports, tree_sitter::{Node, Parser} import dropped
stop_thresholds_status.all_m1_red_tests_still_green: PARTIAL — 133/166 (80.1%) up from M3's 131/166. 2 of 3 M3 Python carry-forward tests transitioned GREEN under canonical: python_path_traversal_positive (M3 RED → M4 GREEN), python_deserialization_positive (M3 RED → M4 GREEN). The third Python test python_xss_positive remains RED for an orthogonal reason — see carry_forwards.M4-CF-01.
stop_thresholds_status.python_specific_e2e_parity_preserved: PASS — all 36 test_e2e_* tests at tldr-core/security/vuln.rs:1568-2100 GREEN, including test_e2e_parameterized_query_no_findings, test_e2e_subprocess_list_no_findings, test_e2e_type_coercion_no_findings, test_e2e_real_sql_injection, test_e2e_real_command_injection (Python primary-regression-guard set)
stop_thresholds_status.fstring_flow_regression_green: PASS — Python f-string interpolation flow exercised by python_sql_injection_positive (cursor.execute(f'... {user_input}')) GREEN under canonical via compute_taint_with_tree's SSA-active-path indirect-match fallback added in M3 (taint.rs Phase 5 SSA-active-path indirect-match — addresses the receiver-shaped sink case the M1 RED test_string_interpolation_detection was scaffolding for).
stop_thresholds_status.python_fp_clean_property_preserved: PASS — binary smoke test on /tmp/vuln-mig-repro/string_literal_fp.py --lang python returns 0 findings (recorded smoke output below)

in_vuln_rs: TaintSource const-pattern struct + PYTHON_SOURCES (11 entries: request.args/form/get/values/data/json/cookies/headers, sys.argv, input(), os.environ) at L114-L210

TaintSink const-pattern struct + PYTHON_SINKS (17 entries: cursor.execute/executemany, raw, os.system/popen, subprocess.run/call/Popen, eval, exec, render_template_string, Markup, open, os.path.join, requests.get/post, urllib.urlopen) at L210-L327
TaintTracker struct + impl (mark_tainted, is_tainted, propagate, expression_is_tainted; MAX_TAINT_DEPTH const) at L335-L390
TaintInfo CLI-local struct (source_desc, source_line, source_column, code_snippet) at L343
analyze_python_file entry point at L730-L745

9 recursive helpers analyze_node + analyze_function + analyze_block + analyze_statement + analyze_assignment + analyze_augmented_assignment + analyze_expression + analyze_call + check_xss_return at L985-L1447
7 support helpers get_python_parser, node_text, is_taint_source, is_taint_sink, is_parameterized_query, is_string_interpolation_tainted, find_taint_in_string at L1454-L1620

4 obsolete unit tests test_is_taint_source, test_is_taint_sink, test_taint_tracker, test_string_interpolation_detection — DELETED inline (M3 precedent: cargo check would fail without deletion since they reference removed symbols; the dispatch-contract's stop-threshold 'cargo check passes' implicitly required this)
Module-doc TIGER-04 reference (TaintTracker max-depth was the relevant tigertrap; canonical compute_taint_with_tree uses its own bounds)
Imports tree_sitter::{Node, Parser} (no longer reachable post-M4)
Rewrite of analyze_file at the post-M4 line range — drops the .py special case, routes Python through the same canonical scan_vulnerabilities branch that handles all 14 other supported languages
Extended doc-comment on analyze_file explaining the M4 collapse rationale and Reframe C carve-out for analyze_rust_file

Retention-rationale comment in tests mod documenting why the 4 obsolete tests were deleted and where their equivalent regression coverage lives now (vuln_migration_v1_red.rs Python f-string flow + canonical taint.rs unit tests + tldr-core/src/security/vuln.rs e2e_test set)
VulnArgs struct + clap derives + VulnArgs::run at L398 (CLI infrastructure unchanged)
collect_files at L528, is_natively_analyzed at L587, is_supported_source_file at L609 (file-walking, language gate, extension routing)

analyze_rust_file at L747 + has_nearby_safety_comment + contains_sql_keyword + is_rust_test_file + 7 rust_finding helpers (Rust line-scanner — UnsafeCode/MemorySafety/Panic/SqlInjection/CommandInjection — distinct concern per Reframe C, permanently out of scope for vuln-migration-v1)
map_core_vuln_type at L718 (already exhaustive per VAL-002 fix; preserved verbatim)

build_summary, format_vuln_text, generate_sarif (output formatting — unchanged, JSON/SARIF schema preserved)
All 6 test_analyze_rust_* unit tests (Rust line-scanner regression guard)
test_vuln_type_cwe_mapping, test_vuln_type_severity (output ontology tests — unchanged)
test_collect_files_includes_rust (file-walking regression guard)

dispatch_contract_deviations.deviation: Dispatch-contract M4 deletions list at line 271 says 'DELETE 5 is/find/vuln_type_name helpers at L1469-L1700'. vuln_type_name was NOT deleted because it is still used by generate_sarif (preserved per the contract) at the rules-array projection — generate_sarif emits {name: vuln_type_name(*vt), shortDescription.text: vuln_type_name(*vt)} for each unique VulnType. Deleting vuln_type_name would break SARIF output (a hard preservation mandate per the contract). The deviation is intentional and the contract's enumerated stop-thresholds (output shape unchanged + SARIF tests GREEN) take precedence over the deletion list.
dispatch_contract_deviations.rationale: Output-shape preservation is a hard mandate (validator_mandates_status.cli_output_shape_unchanged in M3 report). vuln_type_name is a pure VulnType → &'static str mapping with no taint dependency — it's an output-formatting helper, not part of the deleted Python taint machinery.

carry_forwards.title: python_xss_positive remains RED — orthogonal source/sink-bank coverage gap, NOT a Python-path-collapse regression
carry_forwards.test_status:
RED — fixture python/xss_positive.py exercises `response.write('

' + name + '

')` where name = request.args.get('name'). Canonical Python AST sink banks at tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs do not emit `response.write` as an XSS sink (the M2 sink-audit was XSS-conservative for Python — Markup/render_template_string covered, not response.write).
carry_forwards.rationale: This is a continuation of M3-CF-01 (other-language source/sink-bank coverage parity). It is NOT introduced by M4 — pre-M4 the CLI-local PYTHON_SINKS table also did not include `response.write` (verified in deleted L283-L289 — only render_template_string + Markup for XSS), so the fixture would have RED-failed under the pre-M4 path too. The pre-M4 GREEN status (referenced in dispatch-contract M4 stop-threshold 'Python-specific tests transition GREEN') was an OPTIMISTIC assumption made before sink-bank parity was empirically tested. The other 2 Python carry-forward tests (path_traversal_positive, deserialization_positive) DID flip GREEN as predicted (open() + pickle.loads() are both in canonical Python sink banks).
carry_forwards.future_work: Add `response.write` (Flask Response API), `Response.set_data`, and similar XSS-shaped sinks to PYTHON_AST_SINKS at tldr-core/src/security/taint.rs:Xss bank. Deferred to the post-M5 source-bank audit milestone analogous to M2's sink-bank audit — see M3-CF-01 future_work.
carry_forwards.validator_mandate_status: stop_thresholds_partial — 2 of 3 M3 Python carry-forwards closed at M4; 1 remaining gap is sink-bank coverage (orthogonal concern, NOT Python-path-collapse regression)

validator_mandates_status.cli_output_shape_unchanged: MET — VulnFinding/VulnSummary/VulnReport struct shapes unchanged; JSON output preserved; SARIF schema preserved (vuln_type_name retained for SARIF rules.name/shortDescription per M4-DEVIATION-01)
validator_mandates_status.test_e2e_preservation_mandatory: MET — all 36 test_e2e_* tests at tldr-core/security/vuln.rs canonical site GREEN (the primary regression guard)
validator_mandates_status.rust_path_preserved_per_reframe_c: MET — analyze_rust_file untouched, all 6 test_analyze_rust_* tests GREEN
validator_mandates_status.fstring_flow_regression_green: MET — Python f-string interpolation tested through python_sql_injection_positive fixture (cursor.execute(f'...{tainted}')) GREEN under canonical SSA-active-path indirect-match fallback added in M3
validator_mandates_status.loc_delta_estimate_met: EXCEEDED — dispatch-contract M4 estimated -650 LOC; actual -984 LOC (the additional ~330 came from deleting 4 obsolete unit tests inline + dropping unused imports + removing TIGER-04 module-doc references)

post_m4_test_summary.tldr_cli_lib_tests: 1378/1381 passing — 3 failing tests (test_text_format_generic_evidence_shows_numbers, daemon_registry::add_then_find_round_trips, daemon_registry::dead_pid_entries_are_pruned_on_read) are PRE-EXISTING at HEAD 8d25e8c and unrelated to M4 (M4 only modified crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs; bugbot/daemon files untouched per git diff --stat)
post_m4_test_summary.tldr_cli_integration_tests_unrelated_fai: Several test targets (cli_tests, cli_remaining_tests, etc.) have pre-existing failures unrelated to vuln. Verified by checking git diff scope: M4 only modified crates/tldr-cli/src/commands/remaining/vuln.rs.

perf_status: Python perf NOT measured at M4 (M3-CF-02 documents perf gate FAIL as a known structural carry-forward; M4 only changes the CLI-side dispatch shape, not the canonical taint engine perf characteristics). Adding Python to the canonical path means it now incurs the same per-function compute_taint_with_tree dispatch cost as other languages — known structural cost, NOT a new regression introduced by M4.
wave_4_status: M4 LANDED with documented carry-forward (M4-CF-01) and one intentional dispatch-contract deviation (M4-DEVIATION-01). All atomic-commit, output-shape, e2e-preservation, and Python FP-class-closure stop-thresholds MET. Two of three M3 Python carry-forwards closed; one (python_xss_positive) deferred to source-bank audit milestone — same disposition as M3-CF-01.
next_milestone: M5 ATOMIC — depends on M4. Deletes obsolete vuln-migration-v1 unit tests + dead-code sweep + binary verification.
